title: XHTML
content:
### What is XHTML? **XHTML** (EXtensible HyperText Markup Language) is a **stricter** version of HTML based on XML. --- ### HTML vs XHTML | Aspect | HTML5 | XHTML | |--------|-------|-------| | **Case** | Case-insensitive | **Lowercase only** | | **Closing tags** | Optional for some | **Required** for all | | **Quotes** | Optional on attributes | **Required** on attributes | | **Self-closing** | `
` | `
` (space + slash) | | **Errors** | Browser tries to fix | **Page does not render** | --- ### HTML Code (Relaxed) ```html

This is a paragraph

This is another
``` ### XHTML Code (Strict) ```xhtml

This is a paragraph

This is another


``` --- ### XHTML Rules 1. All tags must be **lowercase** 2. All tags must be **closed** (`

` or `
`) 3. All attributes must be **lowercase** 4. All attributes must have **quoted values** 5. **Minimize** boolean attributes (`disabled="disabled"`) 6. All images must have `alt` text 7. The root element must be `` with XML namespace --- ### XHTML Document Structure ```xhtml XHTML Page

Hello, XHTML!

``` --- ### Is XHTML Still Used? - Modern web uses **HTML5** (not XHTML) - XHTML was popular in the 2000s - Some frameworks (like JSX) use XML-style syntax - Not required today, but understanding it helps with strict coding habits > **Note:** XHTML is **not required** for modern web development. HTML5 is the current standard. But following XHTML strictness makes your code cleaner.
codeExample:
Hello World

Hello World


Hello World
